items.summary: Repasamos el primer triunfo de peso de España en las fases de eliminación de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. Además, analizamos cómo evolucionó el equipo respecto a la fase de grupos y el exitoso recorrido de Luis de la Fuente al frente de la Roja.

items.title: Weather conditions again brought devastating floods to a vulnerable swath of Texas
items.summary: Severe storms have dumped over 20 inches of rain in parts of Texas, causing significant flooding

items.title: Iceberg lettuce supplier being investigated by FDA as source of cyclosporiasis outbreak
items.summary: The Food and Drug Administration is investigating an iceberg lettuce supplier as the source of a parasite outbreak causing "explosive" diarrhea in people who contract the illness. Nationwide, nearly 7,000 people may have been sickened, with 1,645 of those cases confirmed and more than 5,100 still under investigation.

items.title: Hegseth backs low-altitude military flyovers as a series of maneuvers draws scrutiny
items.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is supporting low-altitude military flyovers as the maneuvers have faced scrutiny

items.title: 53+ best new products in July from Hoka, Beis, Olaplex and more
items.summary: We rounded up July’s newest tech, home and beauty products. Shop the Hoka Clifton 11, Beis Weekender, Olaplex serum oil mist and more.

items.title: Iceberg lettuce supplier investigated as possible source in cyclosporiasis outbreak
items.summary: The FDA is investigating iceberg lettuce supplied to Taco Bell by Taylor Farms as a source in an escalating outbreak of cyclosporiasis.

items.title: Wife of Colombian father killed by ICE in Maine says they had planned to grow old together
items.summary: Karolina Rojas wanted to make sure that her husband, Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, was remembered the right way after he was fatally shot by an ICE officer in Biddeford, Maine.

items.title: White House says ICE traffic stops will continue after deadly shootings
items.summary: The comments came following widespread reports earlier in the week that those stops would be put on pause after two immigrants were fatally shot by ICE agents in early July.

items.title: Flood sirens blare in southcentral Texas as rivers reach perilous heights
items.summary: Flood sirens blared early Thursday in Comfort, Texas, as the Guadalupe River rose more than 30 feet in three hours, the U.S. Geological Survey said. The storm could bring even more rainfall than last year's deadly Fourth of July floods.

items.title: 3 big Senate fundraising takeaways: From the Politics Desk
items.summary: In today’s edition, we break down the toplines from a fresh batch of campaign finance filings. Plus, Jonathan Allen previews President Donald Trump’s speech tonight from the White House.

items.title: After years of false claims on voting, Trump to give an address on election integrity
items.summary: President Trump, who for years has sowed doubt about the security of American elections, is scheduled to give a primetime address Thursday night about election integrity. Trump has long contended, without evidence, that he won the 2020 election

items.title: Mississippi teachers say new state-mandated process for buying classroom supplies is more difficult
items.summary: Teachers in Mississippi say accessing state funds for classroom supplies has become more difficult with a new state-mandated process

items.title: SpaceX stock closes below its IPO price for the first time since it went public
items.summary: Shares of SpaceX continued their sharp descent Thursday and closed below the company’s IPO price for the first time since its June public offering.

items.title: Ghost gun company ordered to pay $100M in death of Kentucky teen in historic verdict
items.summary: A ghost gun company has been ordered to pay over $100 million in the death of a Kentucky teenager who had purchased the company's pistol-building kit online

items.title: NYC helicopter that crashed, killed 6 last year showed signs of a bird strike: NTSB
items.summary: The National Transportation Safety Board has reported that the remains of several geese were found on the wreckage of a sightseeing helicopter that crashed into the Hudson River last year in New York City and killed 6 people

items.title: Una última ovación para las leyendas del fútbol que dijeron adiós en la Copa Mundial 2026
items.summary: Después de años de títulos y momentos inolvidables, varias de las grandes figuras del futbol disputaron su última Copa Mundial de la FIFA. Recorremos el legado que dejan Cristiano Ronaldo, Neymar, Luka Modrić, James Rodríguez y Guillermo Ochoa.

items.title: Talking Shop with Béis founder Shay Mitchell
items.summary: Béis used customer feedback to redesign its core collection of travel essentials, including The Weekender. Founder Shay Mitchell shares what’s new and what she packs inside.

items.title: 2 executions scheduled for same day in Florida for the first time in more than 60 years
items.summary: The state of Florida is set to execute two death row inmates on the same day for the first time in more than 60 years, now that a stay has been lifted for a former police officer who had been scheduled to die earlier his year

items.title: Tesla driver whose car slammed into home overrode driver assistance mode, NTSB says
items.summary: Weeks after a 76-year-old woman was killed when a Tesla, operating on Full Self-Driving, crashed into her Texas home, the National Transportation Safety Board concluded that the car’s driver overrode the driver assistance mode and accelerated into the building.

items.title: Sen. John Fetterman says he’ll leave the Democratic Party if it ‘officially’ becomes ‘anti-Israel’
items.summary: Sen. John Fetterman said he would leave the Democratic Party if it “officially” becomes “the anti-Israel party.”

items.title: Substance FBI agent saw in van of man fatally shot by ICE in Houston was salt, family attorney says
items.summary: An FBI search warrant application said an agent found small bags in the van that he believed could contain drugs, which was refuted by the local DA and a group representing the victim’s family.

items.title: Why Orthodox Jews are opposing the new daylight saving bill in Congress
items.summary: Orthodox Jewish organizations say the bill, if passed, would force morning prayer services to start after 9 a.m. in some parts of the country, making observant Jews late for work and school.

items.title: Officials probe whether White House teleprompter operator profited off Trump's words
items.summary: It's the first known instance of officials investigating suspected insider trading on a prediction market from inside the White House.

items.title: Police arrest man who shouted racial slur at ‘TODAY’ co-anchor Craig Melvin
items.summary: New York City police arrested a man Thursday morning after he allegedly entered an unauthorized area near the “TODAY” show studio and shouted a racial slur at co-anchor Craig Melvin, according to multiple people familiar with the matter and a statement from NBC News.

items.title: What to know about the SAVE America Act and Trump’s push for voting changes
items.summary: Trump is renewing his push for the SAVE America Act, a bill that would require states to look for noncitizen voters and would force voters to confirm U.S. citizenship when participating in elections.

items.title: US designates 2 new Mexican cartels as foreign terrorist organizations
items.summary: The U.S. government has designated two new Mexican cartels as foreign terrorist organizations

items.title: Hong Kong official warns booksellers on security risks after raids
items.summary: The police operation marks the third round of arrests targeting independent bookstores in four months.

items.title: Trump immediately fires the new court-appointed top prosecutor in Seattle
items.summary: President Donald Trump has fired the new top U.S. prosecutor in Seattle less than an hour after the attorney was unanimously appointed by the federal judges in the district

items.title: Trump wants a permanent fence around the D.C. park known for White House protests
items.summary: The Trump administration wants to install permanent fencing around Lafayette Park, directly outside the White House. It's long been a popular spot for protesters, who worry barriers will change that.

items.title: US expands strikes into northern Iran and disables ship trying to run blockade
items.summary: The United States has intensified its strikes on Iran, hitting targets further north

items.title: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine's tech-savvy defense minister in government reshuffle
items.summary: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired the country's popular defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, who pushed for innovation on the battlefield through use of drones and turned the tables on Russia.

items.title: The political risks from war in Iran. And, ICE's use of force is rising, report finds
items.summary: The political implications the war in Iran is having on the GOP. And, a new report has found that ICE officers' use of force is not rare since President Trump took office for his second term.

items.title: Federal panel reviews fencing plan, screening center to improve White House security
items.summary: Proposals from the Trump administration to improve security around the White House are up for review by the federal agency with oversight over the design

items.title: Oil companies are making billions. In the U.S., calls to tax their windfall are growing
items.summary: Higher oil prices since the Iran war began mean many oil companies have brought in excess profits. Some U.S. lawmakers want to tax those windfall profits and give the money to lower-income Americans.

items.title: US imposing 25% tariff on some Brazilian imports, citing unfair trade practices
items.summary: The United States is imposing 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil after finding a range of what it deemed unfair trade practices by the world’s 10th-biggest economy

items.title: Ukraine targets Russian tankers in the Sea of Azov
items.summary: Video released by Ukraine’s Aerial Systems Forces shows strikes on Russian vessels in the Sea of Azov.

items.title: El abrazo que hizo llorar a todos: Messi y Scaloni unidos por el sueño de la cuarta estrella
items.summary: Tras vencer a Inglaterra y sellar el pase a la Gran Final, el 10 recorrió la cancha abrazando compañeros y cuerpo técnico, pero el momento más emotivo llegó cuando capitán y estratega se fundieron en un cariñoso abrazo que emocionó a todo un país.

items.title: Top officials in Arizona's Maricopa County agree on how to oversee elections, ending a legal battle
items.summary: Election officials in Arizona's most populous county now agree on how to jointly oversee the vote

items.title: Batistuta: "España llega bien porque le ganó a Francia, pero lo de Argentina es épico" | El Pelotazo
items.summary: El exgoleador de la "Albiceleste" valoró la llegada de España a la Final tras vencer a Francia; no obstante, destacó la manera en que la "Scaloneta" remontó dos partidos épicos para llegar tras el triunfo histórico ante Inglaterra.

items.title: Jordan's military says it shot down 8 missiles launched by Iran targeting the kingdom
items.summary: Jordan's military says it shot down 8 missiles launched by Iran targeting the kingdom

items.title: Scaloni: "Ojalá los españoles estén contentos de que Argentina esté en la Final por Messi"
items.summary: El DT campeón del mundo elogió a sus jugadores tras vencer a Inglaterra, defendió a su capitán como el mejor de todos los tiempos y advirtió que aunque tiene familia española, irá a por el título ante España en la Final del domingo.

items.title: Tuchel tras la derrota de Inglaterra: "Argentina jugó con más riesgo; fue merecido"
items.summary: El DT de los “Tres leones” dio la cara tras quedar eliminado ante el campeón del mundo, aseguró que los europeos dieron su mejor partido en la Copa del Mundo y le dio mérito al Campeón del Mundo por remontar el duelo cuando arrancaron en desventaja.

items.title: Argentina beats England to reach finals
items.summary: Argentina will now face Spain in the World Cup final after coming from behind to win today’s match. The reigning champs are now fighting for a chance to win back to back titles. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch has the story.

items.title: Argentina celebra el pase a la final y en Inglaterra asimilan una dolorosa eliminación
items.summary: Giuliano Simeone, Leandro Paredes y Lautaro Martínez compartieron sus sensaciones tras la agónica clasificación de Argentina a la final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. Del lado inglés, Thomas Tuchel analizó la eliminación de Inglaterra.

items.title: Radar Mundial: Argentina firma una remontada épica y va por otra estrella
items.summary: Argentina venció 2-1 a Inglaterra con una remontada memorable y selló su pase a la final del Mundial 2026. Messi lideró a la Albiceleste hacia un histórico duelo frente a España por la Copa del Mundo.

items.title: La personalidad de Argentina la lleva a otra final mundialista
items.summary: Los expertos analizaron cómo Argentina volvió a demostrar carácter para remontar frente a Inglaterra y conseguir su pase a la final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. También destacaron los ajustes del equipo tras el descanso.

items.title: La remontada de Argentina sobre Inglaterra se roba lo mejor del día
items.summary: Argentina vino de atrás para derrotar a Inglaterra y sellar su boleto a la gran final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. La Albiceleste reaccionó con autoridad en la segunda mitad y mostró el carácter que la tiene nuevamente a un paso del título.

items.title: Ex-captain pleads guilty to drugging and raping a US Merchant Marine Academy cadet on a cargo ship
items.summary: A former cargo ship captain has pleaded guilty to drugging and raping a 21-year-old U.S. Merchant Marine Academy cadet who was working on the vessel as part of an academy training program

items.title: Vance says Trump administration 'screwed up' communications around Epstein files
items.summary: Vice President JD Vance says the Trump administration “absolutely” mishandled the communications surrounding the Jeffrey Epstein files

items.title: Muslim man in Utah was targeted in stabbing because of his religion, police say
items.summary: A Utah man told police he repeatedly stabbed a Muslim man because of the man's faith and intended to kill him

items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh on Wednesday sought to evade a range of questions from senators on issues such as the impact of AI on inflation, what contacts he has had with President Donald Trump, and how the central bank will determine the persist...

items.title: Federal government installs reworked panels about slavery at George Washington's Philadelphia home
items.summary: The Trump Administration followed through on its pledge to rework and reinstall panels about slavery at the site of President George Washington’s home in Philadelphia

items.title: China and Xi are seen more favorably than the US and Trump in many nations, new survey says
items.summary: The world has largely viewed the U.S. more favorably than China for years, but those opinions now have flipped in Beijing’s favor

items.title: Rescuers are still searching for 3 people after a boat sank in San Francisco Bay, leaving 1 dead
items.summary: Rescuers are still searching for three people missing after a boat sank in San Francisco Bay near Alcatraz Island

items.title: House Republicans unveil $95 billion plan for the Iran war, farm aid and elections
items.summary: House Republicans have unveiled a $95 billion plan focused on boosting defense, aiding farmers and enacting stricter voter registration rules

items.title: Heavy rains keep drenching South Texas, tornado reported in San Antonio
items.summary: Heavy downpours are still drenching South Texas with the possibility of dangerous flash flooding

items.title: The Latest: Trump says ICE should continue traffic stops despite new policy to halt them
items.summary: President Donald Trump says Immigration and Customs Enforcement should continue traffic stops after two deadly shootings within a week, seeming to contradict a new policy to halt them

items.title: The Latest: Todd Blanche faces US Senate for DOJ confirmation hearing
items.summary: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is confronting questions about his brief but turbulent tenure atop the Justice Department during a Senate confirmation hearing that will test President Donald Trump’s grip on Republican lawmakers whose support the n...

items.title: Wildfires expose millions in the Midwest and Northeast US to dangerous smoke
items.summary: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is expected to engulf large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week, exposing millions of people to dangerous air pollution

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
